commands.review_summary:
The PR defers Gateway channel account startup until sidecar readiness, makes startup model prewarm opt-in with `OPENCLAW_STARTUP_MODEL_PREWARM`, uses the read-only model catalog for provider-auth warmup, and reuses plugin/auth metadata during models.json planning.

Reproducibility: no. independent high-confidence runtime reproduction was established in this read-only review. Source inspection shows the implicated startup work is currently on the readiness path, and the PR body provides after-fix configured Gateway timing evidence.
commands.review_followup: The protected maintainer label and intentional startup default change need maintainer acceptance and upgrade-safety confirmation, not an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix: avoid gateway startup event-loop stalls
target.body:
## Summary

- Defer channel account startup until the Gateway has completed the readiness handoff, so account-level channel work cannot starve HTTP readiness.
- Make startup primary-model prewarm opt-in while keeping provider auth warming on the read-only model catalog path.
- Reuse plugin metadata and provider auth lookup maps during models.json planning so model normalization/auth discovery do not reload manifest/provider auth data for every provider/model.
- Add regression coverage for startup prewarm policy, deferred channel startup, and provider auth catalog loading.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/gateway/server-startup.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/manifest-model-id-normalization.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.auth-provenance.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.auth-aliases.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm check:changed` via Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01ksadggy3vj5wkpnkn4h1ht2w`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Gateway startup no longer lets channel account startup, model prewarm, or provider catalog/auth metadata planning monopolize the event loop before readiness.
Real environment tested: local configured Gateway with Discord, Slack, and Telegram accounts in tmux; local configured provider catalog/models.json profiling.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_STARTUP_TRACE=1 pnpm gateway:watch --force`; `pnpm exec tsx /tmp/profile-models-json-plan.ts`; `pnpm exec tsx /tmp/profile-implicit-provider-details.ts`; `OPENCLAW_DEBUG_INGRESS_TIMING=1 pnpm exec tsx -e '... loadModelCatalog readOnly/full ...'`
Evidence after fix: latest tmux startup showed HTTP listening at 11:44:31, Gateway ready at 11:44:32, channels starting after readiness, and no `fetch-timeout ... timer delayed` warning in the latest run; read-only model catalog is ~190ms; models.json planning dropped from ~75s to ~20s cold, with model-id normalization reduced from ~58s to ~1.4s + ~1ms and provider auth env lookup no longer repeating manifest work for each provider.
Observed result after fix: Discord, Slack, and Telegram startup progressed without the previous 30s event-loop starvation warning; provider auth prewarm finished after readiness; full catalog remains dominated by provider runtime augmentation fanout rather than file IO or pure model-id normalization.
What was not tested: full repository suite locally; PR CI plus Testbox changed checks are the broad proof for this branch.
target.author: steipete
